ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class (FOODON_03311737); no specific houjicha or roasted green tea subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers tea and infusion beverages broadly; no houjicha-specific code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227415007 covers green tea as a substance; houjicha is a roasted variant of green tea. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Pokka Houjicha bottle of 500 ml, equivalent to approximately 500 g, is a typical single-serve portion consumed as a ready-to-drink beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Pokka Houjicha (Roasted Green Tea) — Pokka Pte Ltd product nutrition information panel; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central: Tea, green, brewed (NDB 14355) for general tea nutrient profile
nutrient_source.organization: Pokka Pte Ltd / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Pokka Houjicha is a commercially bottled roasted green tea with negligible macronutrients. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917), which reports near-zero macros consistent with plain brewed tea. Pokka product label confirms 0 kcal per 100 ml, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate. Micronutrient values for potassium and fluoride are estimated from USDA green tea data. All core macros are confirmed zero by product formulation and USDA reference.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; plain tea and non-caloric beverages convention
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Houjicha is a roasted green tea containing no glycemic carbohydrates. No direct GI measurement exists for houjicha in the University of Sydney GI Database. By established convention for non-caloric beverages (plain tea, black coffee, water), glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) and gi_category is set to low. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. Reference: Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (approximately 1 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Pokka Houjicha contains negligible sodium (~5 mg per 500 ml serving) and no added salt, making it a suitable beverage choice for individuals managing hypertension; however, patients should verify the current product label as formulations may vary.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no added sugar in unsweetened formulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pokka Houjicha (unsweetened) contains 0 g carbohydrate and 0 g sugar per 100 ml, producing no postprandial glucose response, making it an appropriate beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; patients should confirm the product is the unsweetened variant before consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of tea polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pokka Houjicha contains no fat, no cholesterol, and no added sugar; roasted green tea polyphenols (catechins and pyrazines) may confer modest cardioprotective antioxidant effects, and the beverage is consistent with a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pokka Houjicha is a commercially bottled, ready-to-drink roasted green tea beverage produced by Pokka Pte Ltd and widely available in Singapore, Japan, and across Southeast Asia. Houjicha (焙茶 / ほうじ茶) is a Japanese tea made by roasting green tea leaves, stems, or stalks over charcoal or in a porcelain pot at high temperature, which converts the green chlorophyll into a reddish-brown colour and transforms the flavour profile from the grassy, vegetal notes of standard green tea into a warm, nutty, toasty, and mildly smoky character with low bitterness. The roasting process also reduces the caffeine content significantly compared to standard green tea, making houjicha a popular choice for children, elderly individuals, and those sensitive to caffeine. Pokka Houjicha is sold in 500 ml PET bottles and contains no added sugar, no calories, no fat, and no protein, making it essentially a flavoured water with trace minerals. From a clinical nutrition perspective, the beverage has a glycemic index effectively equal to zero (set to 1 by convention for non-caloric beverages) and a glycemic load of 0.0, making it entirely safe for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The negligible sodium content (~1 mg per 100 ml) makes it suitable for hypertension management. The absence of saturated fat and cholesterol supports cardiovascular health. Roasted green tea contains pyrazines and residual catechin polyphenols that may provide antioxidant benefits. In Singapore, Pokka Houjicha is commonly consumed as a meal accompaniment, a between-meal refreshment, or a lower-caffeine alternative to coffee and standard green tea.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Pokka Houjicha?
Is Pokka Houjicha suitable for hypertension?
Is houjicha the same as green tea?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central brewed green tea data (FDC ID 171917) and cross-referenced with Pokka product label information. The product label confirms zero macronutrients per 100 ml. No direct entry for Pokka Houjicha exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Confidence is medium because the nutrient profile is well-supported for a zero-calorie tea beverage but relies on adaptation rather than an exact primary-source match for this specific commercial product.
notes: energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is a non-caloric brewed tea beverage containing no glycemic carbohydrates, fat, or protein. protein_g is naturally zero by product formulation (confirmed zero per Pokka product label and USDA FoodData Central brewed green tea reference). fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation (confirmed zero per Pokka product label and USDA FoodData Central). carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this is an unsweetened roasted tea with no added sugars or starch (confirmed zero per Pokka product label). sugar_g is naturally zero; dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero for a brewed liquid tea. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all naturally zero for a non-caloric tea beverage. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for non-caloric beverages. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed green tea source entry. Multilingual names provided for Japanese (mandatory for Japanese-origin product), plus Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to support Singapore market context. Pokka is a Singapore-based beverage company producing Japanese-style teas; the product is sold in Singapore and classified under Japanese cuisine origin. Price from field survey input: SGD 1.80.
